pages.text: Generating power from a reactor requires a multi-stage thermodynamic loop. $(br2)1. Primary Loop: Liquid Sodium is pumped into the Reactor Controller. It absorbs core heat and becomes Hot Liquid Sodium.
2. Heat Exchanger: Hot Sodium is pumped into a Heat Exchanger. It transfers its thermal energy to a secondary water loop, flashing the water into Steam while returning cooled Sodium to the reactor.

3. Steam Turbine: Steam is piped into a Steam Turbine. The turbine converts the thermal energy and pressure of the steam into rotation, generating Forge Energy (FE). Spent steam can be condensed back to water.
The Heat Exchanger is the bridge between the radioactive core loop and the clean power loop.
The Steam Turbine is where the actual electricity is generated.
